entry.title: Washington Post: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Russia closes in on Lysychansk; Zelensky rallies support for E.U. candidacy
  • ‘El Chango’: tributes pour in for Mexican narco pet monkey shot dead
  • These are the journalists killed during Russia’s war on Ukraine
  • Afghanistan earthquake kills at least 920, injures 600, officials say
  • Australian state bans Nazi swastika amid resurgence of far-right extremism
  • U.S. attorney general visits Kyiv as heavy fighting continues in Luhansk
  • Two Jesuit priests in Mexico are slain in area torn by drug trade
  • Netanyahu prepares for a comeback in Israel’s next elections
  • Britain’s trains grind to a halt in biggest rail strike in 30 years
  • Deadly South Asia floods leave families trapped without food and water
  • Russia threatens Lithuania for enforcing E.U. sanctions on Kaliningrad
  • Sunrise rituals and music parties: The world marks the summer solstice
  • American detainees won’t get Geneva Convention protections, Kremlin says
  • Russia’s Muratov sells Nobel medal for $103.5M to help Ukrainian children
  • News agency: 1,000 dead, 1,500 injured in Afghan quake
  • Climate change a factor in 'unprecedented' South Asia floods
  • Pope blasts violence in Mexico as he mourns 2 slain Jesuits
  • Director Haggis appears in Italy court amid assault probe
  • Officials: Militants ambush security post in Yemen; 8 killed
  • 'The impossible': Ukraine's secret, deadly rescue missions
  • South China floods force tens of thousands to evacuate
  • 'It's terrible:' Philadelphia mayor sorry for slain Filipino
  • Jesuit priests killed by gunmen in church in northern Mexico
  • US, Iran in tense sea incident; Tehran preps new centrifuges
  • Minnesota city to pay $3.25M in police killing of Daunte Wright, lawyers say
  • A Tesla was in a junkyard for three weeks. Then it burst into flames.
  • Theater backs off plan to fast-forward through same-sex ‘Lightyear’ kiss
  • In rare medical procedure, these two women share one liver
  • Cousins receive mystery postcards sent decades ago: ‘I was stunned’
  • Armed Uvalde officers waited for key to unlocked door, official says
  • Bill Cosby sexually abused 16-year-old in 1975, jury in civil trial finds
  • The harrowing aftermath of counting votes
  • Clela Rorex, ‘unsung hero’ of marriage equality movement, dies at 78
  • Alone in Washington, Rusty Bowers tells world what happened in Arizona
  • Trump’s pressure drew violence, threats to local officials, committee shows
  • How the Jan. 6 hearings could complicate the upcoming Proud Boys trial
  • Election workers describe ‘hateful’ threats after Trump’s false claims
  • Jan. 6 hearings, Day 4 recap: Panel links Trump to fake electors plan
  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • This inflation-proof bond is paying 9.62%. Here’s how to buy it.
  • Covid bailout rescued some hospitals while enriching others
  • Top executives quit Pornhub’s parent company amid more controversy
  • Crypto’s frozen mystery: The fate of billions in Celsius deposits
  • Supreme Court rejects appeal from Roundup maker over cancer claims
  • Streamers worry the end is coming for lax password-sharing rules
  • The man who saved Final Fantasy is forging its future with ‘FFXVI’
  • Who is responsible for repairing damaged insulation in a condominium?
  • Housing market slowdown takes hold as mortgage rates dig in
  • Luxury touches with D.C.’s bustling Kennedy Street around the corner
  • OPEC+ Mulls When to Fire Its Last Oil Production Bullets
  • The US Economy Is Headed for a Hard Landing
  • For Colombia’s New Leader, Winning Was the Easy Part
  • Italy’s Families Aren’t Rich Enough to Evade a Crisis
  • What ‘Friend-Shoring’ Means for Trade in a Less-Friendly World
  • Why China and US Disagree on Forced Labor in Xinjiang
  • How a British Passport Offers Escape for Hong Kongers: QuickTake
  • How Chip Neutrality Scuppered Nvidia Deal to Buy Arm: QuickTake
  • Business beware, ‘stakeholder’ capitalism is parasitic progressivism
  • A gas puzzler: If Biden ran Big Oil, what would he do differently?
  • Restricting U.S. oil exports would betray European allies and benefit Russia
  • Trump, CNN and ‘the big lie’
  • Think state supreme courts will save abortion rights? Think again.
  • Israel’s election will once again be all about Netanyahu
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 22 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-22 11:08AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Death toll continues to rise after earthquake in eastern Afghanistan
  • AFL dropped in-person training on respect for women
  • Joey lauds Blues' selection 'masterstroke'
  • Perth tourist injured while waiting for taxi
  • Norman’s Saudi-backed defectors allowed to play The Open at St Andrews

  • ‘He is going to fight to his dying breath’: Renowned swimming coach charged over alleged sexual assault

  • Top Texas cop slams response to school shooting
  • Massive lines, painful delays at Heathrow airports

  • Damning evidence slams police response in Uvalde shooting
  • ‘Abject failure’: Police made ‘terrible’ decisions during Uvalde shooting, official says

  • Offensive behaviour to be criminalised after Eastern Freeway tragedy
  • Man who predicted COVID-19 numbers faces court
  • Apology, pardon for WA mother who ‘endured the unthinkable’

  • Family with six kids forced to live in tent
  • Four-year-old boy dies in South Australia crash
  • SA paedophile to spend longer behind bars
  • Dine and discover vouchers set to expire
  • Paul Whyte began his $22 million fraud on second day in the job: CCC

  • Two killed, two injured in horror Brisbane crash
  • Former Sydney swim coach arrested
  • Zip Co tries to reassure market as share price tanks
  • ‘No guiding strategy’: Former Judith Neilson Institute executive unloads
  • ASX falls as US futures weigh on the market, wiping out early gains

  • Grocery delivery startup Voly slashes operations as tech sector slumps

  • The ‘crypto winter’ has set in but it may have a silver lining
  • Crown gets green light to open Sydney casino

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 22 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-22 11:00AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘Glocalised’ school-based curriculum at Caritas Wu Cheng-chung Secondary School empowers innovation and inspires students from nearly 20 countries and regions
  • Hong Kong police arrest 7 for allegedly conspiring with 3 retail outlets to cash in HK$220,000 worth of e-vouchers
  • Hong Kong to include Korean in DSE university entrance exam from 2025 amid K-Culture boom
  • Hong Kong child abuse scandal: first of 27 defendants to plead guilty in care home case gets 4 months’ jail
  • Hong Kong’s incoming deputy justice secretary to quit pro-Beijing group amid concerns membership could undermine impartiality
  • Veteran Hong Kong lawmaker Regina Ip appointed convenor of incoming leader John Lee’s de facto cabinet
  • Power supply ‘fully restored’ in Hong Kong districts hit by worst outage in decade but some estates still waiting for electricity
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong tour group heads to Japan for the first time in more than 2 years after Covid rules eased, while city logs 1,447 cases
  • What happened to Hong Kong’s Jumbo Floating Restaurant? Everything you need to know about the ill-fated tourist attraction
  • Hong Kong’s housing and economic development must be linked to the environment to create a liveable city
  • Unpacking Hong Kong’s laws, 25 years after handover: 5 rulings on Basic Law where Beijing, not the city’s courts, had final say in key cases
  • Hong Kong maritime authorities request report from owner after Jumbo Floating Restaurant sinks in South China Sea, reveal attraction was headed for Cambodia
  • 20,000 Hong Kong households face 2-day blackout after cable fire triggers power outages, class suspensions and disruptions to hospital and rail services
  • 76 per cent of young people polled identify as ‘Hongkongers’, while only 2 per cent think of themselves as ‘Chinese’
  • Tech war: Shenzhen recruits Japanese semiconductor heavyweight as China moves to challenge US, Korean dominance in memory chips
  • China’s Tangshan city stripped of ‘civilised’ status after brutal attack on women diners
  • China’s electricity demand soars as heatwaves bake northern, central provinces
  • China inflation: Li Keqiang says ‘prudent’ economic policy, bumper wheat harvest leaves room to tackle risks
  • US business group calls for ‘urgent’ talks on Taiwan free-trade deal
  • When will China’s Fujian aircraft carrier be ready for active duty?
  • Boris Johnson’s father Stanley hopes UK parliament removes ban on Chinese ambassador
  • China supercomputer achieves global first with ‘brain-scale’ AI model
  • China-US relations: Joe Biden’s Asia-Pacific trade framework to be handled with ‘caution’, Beijing advisers say
  • Beijing sends 29 warplanes into Taiwan’s air defence zone in one of largest fly-bys of 2022
  • China plans to review every single social media comment, sparking more censorship fears
  • China, US urged to ‘start small’ as Biden ponders Trump-era trade tariff rollback amid record inflation
  • In Brussels, tram ads for 25th Hong Kong handover anniversary pulled after complaints
  • Uygur Forced Labour Prevention Act takes effect in US, banning imports from Xinjiang
  • Police officer fired and 5 under investigation over attack on women in Chinese city of Tangshan
  • Chinese warships appear to be sailing around Japan amid tensions
  • China trade: North Korea’s Chinese soybean imports surge as coronavirus outbreak ramps up food shortage fears
  • Chinese province offers more maternity leave to help avert population crisis
  • Singapore’s Covid-19 infections rise amid new subvariants but no change to rules – although health ministry urges boosters
  • Myanmar minister joins Asean defence meeting as junta shifts Suu Kyi trial to prison
  • US$3 billion tax bill for Philippines’ Marcos family will be collected vows new chief – 30 years after dictator’s death
  • US bans use of anti-personnel landmines everywhere – except the Korean peninsula
  • Reef shark rescued from Singapore oil refinery
  • Filipino lawyer shot dead in US was ‘a case of mistaken identity’, official says
  • Why Asia wants more nuclear weapons – thanks to Russia’s war in Ukraine
  • Indonesian artists’ ‘anti-Semitic’ work removed from German show after upsetting Israel
  • Japan’s Kishida says talks with China key to regional peace amid rising maritime tensions
  • From Indonesia to Pacific, women less likely to have 2 Covid-19 vaccine shots than men
  • Singapore and South Korea report first monkeypox cases
  • Syed Saddiq faced ‘pressure’ by Malaysia’s PPBM, his parents say during corruption trial
  • Russia pounds eastern Ukraine as it marks date of Hitler’s invasion
  • Afghanistan earthquake kills 950, toll likely to rise
  • First delivery of German heavy weaponry arrives in Ukraine as Zelensky urges West for more, quickly
  • Fate of ancient Afghan Buddhist city in hands of Taliban, Chinese copper mine consortium
  • Most significant US gun legislation in decades clears hurdle in Senate
  • Inside Ukraine’s bombarded Luhansk region: ‘Everything is on fire’
  • Bill Cosby must pay US$500,000 for Playboy Mansion sex assault in 1975, US jury finds
  • Capitol riot hearings: election officials recount threats, pressure campaign to keep Trump in power
  • Thousands flock to Stonehenge for summer solstice, the longest day of the year
  • Ukraine strikes Black Sea oil platform used as Russian military installation
  • Top French court rules against burkinis in city’s pools
  • Ukraine war: Russia vows to inflict pain on Lithuania over Kaliningrad goods ‘blockade’
  • Ukraine war: Russia says captured US fighters may face death penalty
  • Shanghai Stock Exchange is world’s No 1 IPO venue as Chinese firms return home to counter US delisting threat
  • ESG is not a scam, but much work remains to define and standardise it
  • Hong Kong start-up EcoBricks, backed by Sino Group, upcycles ‘problem plastic’ to reduce landfill use and carbon emissions
  • Bitcoin resumes slide amid mounting concerns about recession
  • Alibaba Health, JD Health pull Chinese healthcare stocks lower amid fears of a crackdown on online pharma platforms
  • CLP Power and HK Electric adapt to help Hong Kong realise 2050 carbon-neutral goal
  • UK developer backed by Hong Kong tycoon Henry Cheng eyes US$171 million in tokenisation plan for London residential tower
  • Hong Kong stock rebound on pause after recouping US$570 billion in market value since March, as health stocks lead decline
  • Private equity fund backed by the family of Hang Seng Bank’s co-founder bets big on China’s SMEs amid fundraising slump
  • Broker exodus: high rents and electronic trading have seen more than half of Hong Kong’s brokerages leave Central since 1997
  • Swire Properties pays HK$1.96 billion for 13,200 sq ft plot in Wan Chai, beating 20 rivals in fierce competition
  • Chinese consumers exhibit more caution and price sensitivity as zero-Covid strategy fans jobs and income uncertainty: survey
  • Singapore’s CapitaLand offers ‘buy now, pay later’ plan for Vietnamese residential project to entice Hong Kong investors
  • Alibaba sales for 618 shopping festival rise with growing appetite for camping, fishing goods
  • Ukraine conflict highlights the need to find better ways to feed the world
  • Turmoil in global market for blockchain-based DeFi products leaves investors holding the bag
  • Chinese EV start-up Li Auto launches L9, the ‘full-size SUV with no match on the market’
  • As it turns 22, CEO says HKEX must learn from tech firms to deliver new products and services at low cost
  • Microsoft scraps some AI facial-analysis tools, citing risk of bias
  • Look Up movie review: bland and patronising stories of grown-up children chasing their dreams hardly make for a celebration of 25 years of Chinese rule in Hong Kong
  • The best seafood, tempura and casual French fare in Hong Kong – a wine expert’s picks
  • No 3-Michelin-star restaurants for Dubai as the food guide gives its first awards to the city
  • Scandinavian design from living room to bedrooms and kitchen turns Hong Kong family apartment into a future-proofed home
  • 5 Hong Kong influencers reveal vulnerable sides – and their tips to overcome tough times and achieve holistic wellness
  • What is skin purging and how is it different from a breakout? Experts explain how to understand what’s happening to your face
  • How volunteering benefits your mental health and self-esteem as well as others: Hong Kong volunteers’ stories
  • BTS taking a break is one of the biggest entertainment news stories of 2022. Why did it get so messy?
  • The Karate Kid musical’s Mr Miyagi on Asian representation, how it’s ‘not a movie being staged’, and stepping into Pat Morita’s shoes
  • How reading The Art of Retail Buying taught co-founder of sustainable fashion label Allegory everything she needed to know about business
  • Who is Lady Sarah Chatto, Queen Elizabeth’s beloved niece? The low-key British royal is the daughter of Princess Margaret, and she attended the Platinum Jubilee with Meghan Markle and Prince Harry
  • Who is Elon Musk’s teen transgender daughter, Vivian Jenna Wilson? Formerly known as Xavier, the 18-year-old is changing her name and gender – and does not ‘wish to be related to’ her billionaire dad
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Fendi presented denim, fringe and rubber sliders for its spring/summer 2023 collection, with an earthy colour palette in abundance
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Versace invoked pop Baroque style with a sustainable pull for its spring/summer 2023 collection, with Donatella Versace at the helm
  • Who is Blackpink’s richest member? Net worths, ranked – Jennie, Lisa, Rosé and Jisoo work with Celine, Prada, Bulgari, Dior and Mac, raking in millions from Instagram and their music too
  • How much does Shah Rukh Khan charge to appear at events? The Bollywood legend performed at Isha Ambani’s wedding, but it could cost you a cool US$1 million to have him attend yours
  • Blue Ivy Carter and Harper Beckham at age 10: how do their childhoods compare? Besides famous parents Beyoncé and Jay-Z, and Posh and Becks, they are both budding fashionistas born into major wealth
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • IEA chief warns Europe to prepare for total shutdown of Russian gas exports
  • ‘Kamikaze’ drone strike hits oil refinery in southern Russia
  • Germany/coal: rising emissions leave politicians doubly compromised
  • The corporate feud over satellites that pitted the west against China
  • The big mistakes of the anti-globalisers
  • Anarchy is a likelier future for the west than tyranny
  • Live news updates: UK house price growth pushes average above £280,000 for first time
  • Stock markets turn lower on concerns over economic outlook
  • Jay Powell faces test of Fed’s ‘unconditional’ resolve to tame inflation

entry.title: Guardian: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Netherlands v England: third ODI – live!

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
